page.title: Map how the external economy works
page.description: Classify knowledge with five dimensions, read relevance as decision distance, and use the heatmap to spot blind spots.

classification.title: 5-Dimensional Cognition Map
classification.description: Classify by five dimensions; relevance stays attached to each item instead of becoming a main filter.

domain.title: Domain
domain.description: Which part of economic operation this belongs to.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: From recognizing the term to having your own framework.

discipline.title: Discipline
discipline.description: Separate economics from sociology before drilling into details.

relevance.title: Decision Distance
relevance.description: Relevance is not a main filter; it shows whether this knowledge is useful for current decisions.

heatmap.title: Domain × Confidence
heatmap.stackedDescription: See where a domain is genuinely understood and where it is only familiar as a term.

entries.title: Knowledge List
entries.description: Each item always shows confidence and decision distance.

gaps.title: Knowledge Gaps
gaps.description: Name the areas that are still only familiar or not yet framed.

prompts.title: Review Prompts
prompts.description: Use these as a monthly check-in for the cognition map.

review.title: Priority Repairs
review.description: Short decision distance plus shallow confidence is the first place to study.

empty.entries: No knowledge entries match the current filters.
empty.topicArea: No entries match {{topic}} under the current filters.
empty.gaps: No knowledge gaps match the current filters.
empty.prompts: No review prompts match the current filters.
empty.review: No priority study items match the current filters.
empty.economics: No economics entries match the current filters.
empty.sociology: No sociology entries match the current filters.

edit.entryDescription: Each entry needs domain, layer, confidence, source, and decision distance.
edit.historyPlaceholder: 2026-03-04 | 听过名词 -> 知道大致逻辑 | Finished a reading set

validation.entryRequired: Please enter a title and summary
validation.history: Revision history must use: date | old level -> new level | trigger
validation.sourceRefs: Source format must be: source label | https://example.com
validation.gapRequired: Please enter the gap and next step

economics.title: Economics
economics.description: Organize decision-relevant knowledge about prices, markets, policy, firms, and macro mechanisms.

sociology.title: Sociology
sociology.description: Understand how people act inside groups and institutions through structure, mobility, organizations, cities, and norms.

discipline.mapDescription: See where this discipline already has a framework and where it is still just a familiar term.

graph.title: Knowledge Links
graph.description: Put economics and sociology on the same relationship map to see how concepts explain one another.
graph.helper: Drag nodes to re-read the structure, click a node for details, and click empty space to clear selection.
graph.detailDescription: The card on the right follows the selected node and translates the map position back into readable context.
graph.emptySelection: Select a node to see its details here.
graph.conceptDescription: This concept works like a junction, pulling scattered knowledge entries back into one shared map.

metrics.entriesDetail: Knowledge entries that already point to related concepts
metrics.nodesDetail: Knowledge nodes plus concept nodes together
metrics.linksDetail: Connections that point from entries to concepts
